admin_panel.notes: The default password is blank — leave the password field empty. If that doesn't work, try 'admin' as the password.

ethernet.pppoe_clear_note: If you've been using this modem with another internet provider, the old settings may still be saved. Change 'My Internet Connection is' to 'Dynamic IP (DHCP)' and clear any username and password fields.
steps_ipoe: Connect an Ethernet cable from the nbn connection box (UNI-D1 port) to the yellow WAN port on the back of the DSL-2888A
Open a web browser and go to http://192.168.1.1 (or http://dlinkrouter.local)
Log in with username 'admin' and leave the password blank (or enter the password you set during initial setup)
Click 'Settings' in the top menu bar, then click 'Internet' in the left sidebar
Under 'WAN Access Type', select 'Ethernet'
Set 'My Internet Connection is' to 'Dynamic IP (DHCP)'
Ensure 'Enable Interface' is checked
Make sure 'VLAN Tagged' is NOT checked (Belong does not use VLAN tagging)
Click 'Save'
Wait 1-2 minutes for the modem to obtain an IP address from the nbn connection box

dsl.pppoe_clear_note: If you've been using this modem with another internet provider, you may see their connection listed on this page. Delete it and create a new one set to 'Dynamic IP (DHCP)'.
steps_ipoe: Connect a phone cable from the telephone wall socket to the grey DSL port on the back of the DSL-2888A (use the supplied micro-splitter if you have a phone on the same line)
Open a web browser and go to http://192.168.1.1 (or http://dlinkrouter.local)
Log in with username 'admin' and leave the password blank (or enter the password you set during initial setup)
Click 'Settings' in the top menu bar, then click 'Internet' in the left sidebar
Under 'WAN Access Type', select 'VDSL'
If an existing PPPoE connection is shown, click on it and delete it
Set 'My Internet Connection is' to 'Dynamic IP (DHCP)'
Ensure 'Enable Interface' is checked
Make sure 'VLAN Tagged' is NOT checked (Belong does not use VLAN tagging)
Click 'Save'
Wait 2-3 minutes for DSL sync and internet connection to establish

wan_config.vlan_nav_path: Settings > Internet (within the WAN connection settings)
wan_config.vlan_notes: VLAN tagging is available as a checkbox within the internet connection settings. Belong does not require VLAN tagging — ensure it is unchecked. Some NZ ISPs require VLAN ID 10 but this does not apply to Australian nbn services.

physical.wan_port_position: Ethernet port on the far right when viewed from rear, separated from the LAN ports
physical.dsl_port_position: RJ11 port on the right side of the rear panel, next to the power jack
physical.reset_button: Recessed pinhole on the side of the device

factory_reset.method: Use a pin or paperclip to press and hold the recessed reset button on the side of the device for more than 10 seconds until all front LEDs turn on, then release. The modem will reboot to factory defaults.
factory_reset.notes: Factory reset restores admin/(blank password) and erases all settings including Wi-Fi name/password and ISP configuration. The setup wizard will run again on first access after reset.

firmware.notes: D-Link DSL-2888A does NOT support automatic firmware checking — firmware must be downloaded manually from the D-Link AU support site and uploaded via the admin panel. Product reached EOL on 31 December 2024; firmware updates may no longer be released.

setup.setup_notes: The DSL-2888A (Python) is a modem-router with both Ethernet WAN and built-in VDSL2/ADSL2+ modem, so it works on all nbn tech types. Key detail: the admin IP is 192.168.1.1, NOT 192.168.0.1 (which is D-Link's default for non-DSL routers). The D-Link admin UI uses 'Settings > Internet' for WAN configuration, where you select the WAN Access Type (Ethernet for FTTP/FTTC/HFC, VDSL for FTTN/B) and then the connection type (Dynamic IP for Belong). On FTTN/B, if the device was previously configured for PPPoE (e.g. from TPG), the existing DSL connection may need to be deleted before creating a new Dynamic IP one. For HFC connections, the nbn NTD may need to be powered off for up to 30 minutes to release the old DHCP lease before the new modem can connect.

setup_sources.description: Lightning IP — DSL-2888A FTTN/B setup guide with step-by-step instructions and screenshots
Simply Broadband NZ — DSL-2888A Ethernet WAN configuration with exact field values (VLAN settings are NZ-specific)
ManualsLib — DSL-2888A User Manual page 37: Dynamic IP (DHCP) configuration details
Modemly — DSL-2888A default login credentials and access instructions
Whirlpool — DSL-2888A settings discussion for nbn FTTN/B connections

setup_confidence.notes: Score: 100 - 5 (default password ambiguity: brand default says blank, some sources say admin/admin, LIP guide says no password — documented all three in notes) - 5 (WAN port color not explicitly confirmed by photos or manual diagram — yellow inferred from D-Link product convention and retailer photos) - 5 (DSL 'delete and recreate' flow not fully documented with per-step screenshots — inferred from LIP guide and Whirlpool discussion) - 10 (EOL product, no further firmware updates, admin UI may vary across firmware versions) = 75.
